Home / Services / Land Clearing

Land Clearing

Efficient and environmentally-conscious land clearing for construction and development projects.

VK Tree & Stump Service provides comprehensive land clearing services for residential and commercial development projects in Minneapolis and the surrounding areas. Whether you're building a new home, expanding your business, or creating a new park, we can prepare the land for construction in a safe and efficient manner.

Our land clearing process includes tree removal, stump grinding, brush removal, and site grading. We utilize specialized equipment to minimize environmental impact and ensure compliance with all local regulations. We are experienced in clearing land for new developments in Eden Prairie, subdivisions in Plymouth, and commercial projects in downtown Minneapolis.

We work closely with developers, contractors, and homeowners to understand their specific needs and provide customized land clearing solutions. Our goal is to prepare your land for construction on time and within budget.

Our Process

  1. 01 Step 1: Site assessment and planning
  2. 02 Step 2: Tree removal and stump grinding
  3. 03 Step 3: Brush removal and site grading
  4. 04 Step 4: Debris removal and cleanup

Benefits

  • Efficient land preparation for construction
  • Environmentally-conscious practices
  • Compliance with local regulations
  • Customized solutions for your specific needs

Frequently Asked Questions

Do I need a permit for land clearing?

Yes, in most cases. Depending on the size and location of the project, you may need to obtain permits from the city, county, or state. We can assist you in navigating the permitting process.

How long does land clearing take?

The duration of land clearing depends on the size and complexity of the project. We will provide you with a realistic timeline during the initial consultation.

What do you do with the cleared vegetation?

We recycle as much of the cleared vegetation as possible. The wood can be processed into lumber, firewood, or mulch, and the debris is often taken to a composting facility.

Get a Free Estimate

Ready to get started with land clearing? Contact us today.
